title: Subnational population estimates: At 30 June 2019 (provisional) | Stats NZ
content:
## Subnational population estimates: At 30 June 2019 (provisional)
22 October 2019, 10:45am
Subnational population estimates give the best available measure of the population, by age and sex, usually living in New Zealand's 16 regions, 67 territorial authority areas, 21 Auckland local board areas, and other areas.
Population estimates are based on geographic boundaries as at 30 June 2019.
Revised 30 June 2018 and provisional 30 June 2019 subnational population estimates are consistent with the revised national series and are based on the 2013 Census usually resident population count, but reflect the 2018 Census population distribution.
The national estimated resident population at 30 June 2014–19 is also based on the 2013 Census usually resident population count and has been revised using the new outcomes-based migration measure (see Migration Data Transformation project ).
Summary of changes to Subnational population estimates: At 30 June 2019 (provisional) has more information.
These population estimates will be revised again in March 2020 to incorporate 2018 Census and 2018 post-enumeration survey results, and updated birth, death, and migration estimates.
After each census, we revise the intercensal population estimates to be consistent with the latest census-base estimated resident population, which corrects for any differences in estimated population change.
- Estimated annual population change by territorial authority year to 30 June 2019 – map data Comma Separated Values, 2 KB

### Subnational population estimates: At 30 June 2019 (provisional)
Excel spreadsheet, 94 KB
In the June 2019 year, estimates of the population usually living in each area indicate:
- All 16 of New Zealand’s regions experienced population growth.
- Northland (2.3 percent), Waikato (2.1 percent), and Bay of Plenty (2.0 percent) regions grew faster than the national average (1.6 percent).
- West Coast (0.2 percent), and Marlborough, Southland, and Gisborne (all with 0.8 percent) were the slowest-growing regions.
- Auckland made up 33 percent of New Zealand’s population, and accounted for 32 percent of total population growth.
- All but two territorial authority areas had population growth. Waitomo and Buller districts had population decreases of 0.9 and 0.3 percent, respectively.
- The fastest-growing territorial authority areas were Selwyn (5.3 percent), Central Otago (3.9 percent), and Queenstown-Lakes (3.7 percent) districts.
- Three territorial authority areas had small natural decreases (less than 100), where deaths outnumbered births. They are Thames-Coromandel, Kapiti Coast, and Buller districts.

### Revision of the 2018 estimates
Consistent with the recent revised National population estimates : At 30 June 2019, the subnational population estimates for 2018 have also been revised. These differ from the originally published 2018 estimates by about 44,500 across New Zealand.
The 2019 subnational resident population estimates now incorporate the outcomes-based measure of migration.
Of the 67 territorial authority areas, 11 were revised down with the largest revisions to Auckland (-77,400), Christchurch (-8,300), Wellington (-7,400), and Hamilton (-3,500) cities. The largest upwards revisions were to Tauranga city (5,800), Hastings district (3,300), the Far North and Western Bay of Plenty districts (both with 2,800).
Percent difference between original and revised 2018 subnational population estimates for territorial authority areas, at 30 June 2019

Queenstown-Lakes district
Within Auckland, the largest numerical revision was to the Waitemata local board area with a decrease of 28,300. This was followed by Albert-Eden and Howick local board areas with decreases of 10,100 and 8,300, respectively.
Percent difference between original and revised 2018 subnational population estimates for Auckland local board areas, at 30 June 2019
Subnational population estimates tables are available in our free online tools Infoshare and NZ.Stat.
For 2018 and 2019 subnational population estimates, select the following categories from Infoshare :
Subject category: Population Group: Population estimates – DPE
Or access population estimates in NZ.Stat :
Subnational population estimates (RC, SA2)
Subnational population estimates (TA, SA2)
Subnational population component changes and median age (RC, TA)
Note: the 2014–17 subnational estimates are temporarily unavailable online as they are inconsistent with the revised series. They are available on request from info@stats.govt.nz . The revised series will be available on 30 March 2020.

## Definitions and metadata
Subnational population estimates – DataInfo+ gives the general methodology used to produce subnational population estimates statistics.

This release will include revised subnational population estimates, June 2014 to June 2019 (annual series).
